_comment: mcpconform (https://github.com/cejor6/mcpconform) lints this server's MCP tool surface in CI; targets set the provider profiles to check against. No per-rule suppressions are needed: the CI step runs with --min-severity warn, so advisory info-tier findings don't clutter the gate -- including the two that flag known, intentional behavior here: tool/meta-namespacing (FastMCP injects a non-reverse-DNS '_meta.fastmcp' key on every tool, which this project doesn't control) and provider/schema-unenforced-keyword (tool params carry minimum/maximum as deliberate LLM-steering, see AGENTS.md 'How to add a new tool', backstopped by authoritative runtime guards). Any warn/error finding is still reported, and error-tier fails the build. Run `npx mcpconform inspect -- uv run kalshi-mcp` locally (no floor) to see info-tier findings; mcpconform >=0.2.0 auto-collapses framework-injected noise to a single line by default.
